text: When working with APIs, you communicate with a remote server using HTTP requests such as GET or POST. Most modern APIs return data in JSON format, which is easy to read and work with in code. Many APIs require authentication, usually via an API key, to ensure only authorised users have access. It's also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a given time. Finally, always include error handling in your code to manage issues like network failures or invalid responses, making your application more reliable.
